info.title: Brand Management API
info.description:
Use the Brand Management REST API to create a [brand kit](https://doc.sitecore.com/stream/en/users/sitecore-stream/brand-kits.html), retrieve all brand kits or a specific one including sections and subsections, and create or update the content of individual subsections.

 This REST API lets you interect with:

 - The brand kit object. A [brand kit](https://doc.sitecore.com/stream/en/users/sitecore-stream/brand-kits.html) is a structured resource that enables organizations to manage their brand across various channels, teams, and content types. It defines the brand's identity through guidelines, tone, messaging rules, and other defining traits.

 After creating a brand kit using the Brand Management REST API, you can use the [Documents REST API](https://api-docs.sitecore.com/ai-skills/ai-document-management-rest-api) to upload brand documents and the [Pipeline REST API](https://api-docs.sitecore.com/ai-skills/ai-pipeline-rest-api) to initiate the brand ingestion process. 

Note the following:

 - To use this REST API, you must authenticate your API requests.

 - All API requests are made in your production environment.

For more information, see the [official Sitecore documentation](https://doc.sitecore.com/).

# Authorization
The Brand Management REST API uses the OAuth 2.0 standard with [JSON web tokens](https://doc.sitecore.com/stream/en/users/sitecore-stream/generate-a-json-web-token--jwt-.html) to authorize REST API requests.
### Create Client ID and Client Secret
  1. In the Sitecore Cloud Portal, open Stream.
  2. Click **Admin** > **AI APIs keys** > **Create credential**.
  3. In the **​Create New Client​​** dialog, enter a name and description for the client. Then click **Create**. The ​Client ID and Client Secret​​ display.
  4. Copy the Client ID and Client Secret because you won't be able to view them again in Stream. You'll use them to request an access token.

### Request an access token
Run the following cURL command to request an access token. Replace the placeholder values with your Client ID and Client Secret.
```curl
  curl -X POST 'https://auth.sitecorecloud.io/oauth/token' \
  --header 'Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded' \
  --data-urlencode 'client_id={YOUR_API_KEY}' \
  --data-urlencode 'client_secret={YOUR_API_SECRET}' \
  --data-urlencode 'grant_type=client_credentials' \
  --data-urlencode 'audience=https://api.sitecorecloud.io'
```
In the response, the `access_token` key contains the access token:
```json
  {
    "access_token": "{YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN}",
    "scope": "ai.org.brd:w ai.org.brd:r ai.org.docs:w ai.org.docs:r ai.org:admin",
    "expires_in": 86400,
    "token_type": "Bearer"
  }
```
Access tokens expire in 24 hours. If your requests unexpectedly return a response with status `401 Unauthorized`, request a new access token by repeating this `POST` request.

We recommend that you cache the access token for 24 hours to avoid repeating this `POST` request while the access token is still valid.
### Include the access token in the request header
You can now start making REST API requests. You must include the access token in the request header of every request. For example:

  ```curl
  curl -X GET '{YOUR_BASE_URL}/v2/...' \
  -H 'Authorization: Bearer {YOUR_ACCESS_TOKEN}' \
  -H 'Accept: application/json'
  ```

get.description: Retrieves a list of brand kits in an organization. The response includes basic information about each brand kit, such as its name, description, industry, and status. The response does not include the actual content of the brand kit sections or subsections.

schema.title: Organizationid
schema.description:
The unique identifier of your organization.

 To get this value, in the [Sitecore Cloud Portal](https://portal.sitecorecloud.io/) URL, the `organizationId` comes after `organization=` .

schema.title: Pagenumber
schema.description: The page number of the brand kits list to retrieve.

schema.title: Pagesize
schema.description: The number of brand kits to retrieve per page.

schema.title: Parentid
schema.description: The parent brand kit ID. Used when AI creates a new version of the brand kit during brand ingestion to indicate the original brand kit it's based on.

schema.title: Status
schema.description: The current status of the brand kit, such as `draft`, `inprocess`, `published`, or `failed`.

schema.title: Search
schema.description: A search term to filter brand kits by name or description.

schema.title: Referencetype
schema.description: The type of reference to filter brand kits by.

schema.title: Referenceid
schema.description: The unique identifier of the brand kit reference to filter brand kits by.

schema.title: Includedeleted
schema.description: Whether to include deleted brand kits in the response.

schema.title: Brandkitids
schema.description: A list of brand kit IDs to filter the results by.
The field to sort the brand kits by.
The sort order to sort the brand kits by.

post.description:
Creates a new brand kit for an organization. The created brand kit includes predefined sections like *Brand Context*, *Global Goals*, *Tone of Voice*, and more, which are empty by default.
 
 In the response, the `id` key contains the created brand kit's unique identifier.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of your organization.

 To get this value, in the [Sitecore Cloud Portal](https://portal.sitecorecloud.io/) URL, the `organizationId` comes after `organization=`.

schema.title: Organizationid
schema.description:
The unique identifier of your organization.

 To get this value, in the [Sitecore Cloud Portal](https://portal.sitecorecloud.io/) URL, the `organizationId` comes after `organization=`.

get.description:
Retrieves a specific brand kit by its ID. The response includes basic information about the brand kit, such as its name, description, industry, and status. The response does not include the actual content of the brand kit sections or subsections.

 If you don't know the brand kit ID, you can use the [List brand kits](#operation/list_brand_kits) endpoint to retrieve the list of brand kits in your organization.

schema.description:
The unique identifier of your organization.

 To get this value, in the [Sitecore Cloud Portal](https://portal.sitecorecloud.io/) URL, the `organizationId` comes after `organization=` .

The unique identifier of your brand kit.

 To get this value, use the [List brand kits](#operation/list_brand_kits) endpoint  to return the `id` of each brand kit in your organization. This is the same `id` returned when you create a brand kit using the [Create a brand kit](#operation/create_brand_kit) endpoint.

 Alternatively, you can open your brand kit in Stream. In the [Sitecore Cloud Portal](https://portal.sitecorecloud.io/) URL,  `brandkitId` comes after `brandkits/`.
Whether to include deleted brand kits in the response.
schema.title: Includedeleted

get.description:
Retrieves a list of sections in a brand kit. Each section contains information that define the brand's identity and guidelines, such as *Brand Context*, *Global Goals*, and *Tone of Voice*.

 To return actual content in the sections, the [Documents REST API](https://api-docs.sitecore.com/ai-skills/ai-document-management-rest-api) and [Pipeline REST API](https://api-docs.sitecore.com/ai-skills/ai-pipeline-rest-api) must have already been used to upload and process brand documents for the brand kit.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of your organization.

 To get this value, in the [Sitecore Cloud Portal](https://portal.sitecorecloud.io/) URL, the `organizationId` comes after `organization=` .

parameters.name: brandkitId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of your brand kit.

 To get this value, use the [List brand kits](#operation/list_brand_kits) endpoint  to return the `id` of each brand kit in your organization. This is the same `id` returned when you create a brand kit using the [Create a brand kit](#operation/create_brand_kit) endpoint.

 Alternatively, you can open your brand kit in Stream. In the [Sitecore Cloud Portal](https://portal.sitecorecloud.io/) URL,  `brandkitId` comes after `brandkits/`.

parameters.name: sectionIds
parameters.description: An array of section IDs to filter the results. If not provided, all sections in the brand kit are returned.

parameters.name: includeDeleted
parameters.description: Whether to include deleted sections in the response.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of your organization.

 To get this value, in the [Sitecore Cloud Portal](https://portal.sitecorecloud.io/) URL, the `organizationId` comes after `organization=` .

parameters.name: brandkitId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of your brand kit.

 To get this value, use the [List brand kits](#operation/list_brand_kits) endpoint  to return the `id` of each brand kit in your organization. This is the same `id` returned when you create a brand kit using the [Create a brand kit](#operation/create_brand_kit) endpoint.

 Alternatively, you can open your brand kit in Stream. In the [Sitecore Cloud Portal](https://portal.sitecorecloud.io/) URL,  `brandkitId` comes after `brandkits/`.

parameters.name: sectionId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of your brand kit section.

 To get this value, use the [List brand kit sections](#operation/list_brand_kit_sections) endpoint. The `id` in the response is the section ID.

get.description:
Retrieves a list of subsections (fields) in a specific brand kit section. Each subsection contains guidelines that define that section's content. For example, the *Brand Context* section has subsections to define the brand's purpose, history, and more. 

 To return actual content in the subsections, the [Documents REST API](https://api-docs.sitecore.com/ai-skills/ai-document-management-rest-api) and [Pipeline REST API](https://api-docs.sitecore.com/ai-skills/ai-pipeline-rest-api) must have already been used to upload and process brand documents for the brand kit.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of your organization.

 To get this value, in the [Sitecore Cloud Portal](https://portal.sitecorecloud.io/) URL, the `organizationId` comes after `organization=` .

parameters.name: brandkitId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of your brand kit.

 To get this value, use the [List brand kits](#operation/list_brand_kits) endpoint  to return the `id` of each brand kit in your organization. This is the same `id` returned when you create a brand kit using the [Create a brand kit](#operation/create_brand_kit) endpoint.

 Alternatively, you can open your brand kit in Stream. In the [Sitecore Cloud Portal](https://portal.sitecorecloud.io/) URL,  `brandkitId` comes after `brandkits/`.

parameters.name: sectionId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of your brand kit section.

 To get this value, use the [List brand kit sections](#operation/list_brand_kit_sections) endpoint. The `id` in the response is the section ID.

schema.title: Response List Brand Kit Section Fields

get.summary: List brand kit subsections with pagination
get.description: Retrieves a paginated list of subsections (fields) in a specific brand kit section, including any associated reference data linked to those fields.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of your organization.

 To get this value, in the [Sitecore Cloud Portal](https://portal.sitecorecloud.io/) URL, the `organizationId` comes after `organization=` .

parameters.name: brandkitId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of your brand kit.

 To get this value, use the [List brand kits](#operation/list_brand_kits) endpoint  to return the `id` of each brand kit in your organization. This is the same `id` returned when you create a brand kit using the [Create a brand kit](#operation/create_brand_kit) endpoint.

 Alternatively, you can open your brand kit in Stream. In the [Sitecore Cloud Portal](https://portal.sitecorecloud.io/) URL,  `brandkitId` comes after `brandkits/`.

parameters.name: sectionId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of your brand kit section.

 To get this value, use the [List brand kit sections](#operation/list_brand_kit_sections) endpoint. The `id` in the response is the section ID.

schema.title: Pagenumber
schema.description: The page number to retrieve. Defaults to 1. Minimum value is 1.

schema.title: Pagesize
schema.description: The number of results per page. Defaults to 50. Minimum value is 1. Maximum value is 100.

patch.summary: Partially update a brand kit subsection
patch.description:
Partially updates a specific subsection (field) in a brand kit.

 A brand kit includes default sections such as *Brand Context*, *Global Goals*, and *Tone of Voice*. Each section contains predefined subsections that reflect your brand guidelines.

 Use this endpoint to update the properties of a subsection, such as intent, content, and AI editability. You can also use this endpoint to delete locale term values from the **Glossary and Localization** section.

parameters.name: organizationId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of your organization.

 To get this value, in the [Sitecore Cloud Portal](https://portal.sitecorecloud.io/) URL, the `organizationId` comes after `organization=` .

parameters.name: brandkitId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of your brand kit.

 To get this value, use the [List brand kits](#operation/list_brand_kits) endpoint  to return the `id` of each brand kit in your organization. This is the same `id` returned when you create a brand kit using the [Create a brand kit](#operation/create_brand_kit) endpoint.

 Alternatively, you can open your brand kit in Stream. In the [Sitecore Cloud Portal](https://portal.sitecorecloud.io/) URL,  `brandkitId` comes after `brandkits/`.

parameters.name: sectionId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of your brand kit section.

 To get this value, use the [List brand kit sections](#operation/list_brand_kit_sections) endpoint. The `id` in the response is the section ID.

parameters.name: fieldId
parameters.description:
The unique identifier of the brand kit subsection you want to update. 

 To get this value, use the [List brand kit subsections](#operation/list_brand_kit_section_fields) endpoint. The `id` in the response is the subsection ID.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the subsection to be created.

order.title: Order
order.description: The position of the subsection within the brand kit section. For example, in the *Global Goals* section, the `order` is `1` for the *Digital mandatories* subsection.

value.title: Value
value.description: The value or content of the created subsection.
value.example: Essential requirements for adapting digital content to diverse linguistic, cultural, and regional contexts.

intent.title: Intent
intent.description: The intent or prompt that instructs the AI in populating this subsection and retrieving information from existing brand knowledge based on uploaded brand documents.
intent.example: The core reason for the brand existence.

references.title: References
references.description: An array of references associated with the brand kit subsection.

deletable.title: Deletable
deletable.description: Whether the subsection can be deleted.

verified.title: Verified
verified.description: Whether the subsection's content has been verified by the user.

aiEditable.title: Aieditable
aiEditable.description: Whether the subsection can be edited by AI. If set to `true`, the AI will overwrite the subsection's content value when brand documents are reprocessed.

createdBy.description: The user who created the subsection.

value.description: The content of the subsection in the brand kit section. This can be a string, an array, or a rich text object.
value.example: The core reason for the brand existence is to fuel the potential of active individuals with clean, nutrient-dense energy solutions.

intent.title: Intent
intent.description: The intent or prompt that instructs the AI in populating this subsection and retrieving information from existing brand knowledge based on uploaded brand documents.
intent.example: The core reason for the brand existence.

references.title: References
references.description: An array of references used in this subsection. Each reference points to a brand document that provides context or content for the subsection.

ParentTypeEnum.title: ParentTypeEnum
ParentTypeEnum.description: The type of the parent entity that the tags are associated with. This can be used to categorize tags based on the specific entity they are linked to, such as a brand kit, section, or subsection.

SortByEnum.title: SortByEnum
SortByEnum.description: The field by which to sort the brand kits. This can be based on the creation date (`createdOn`) or the last updated date (`updatedOn`).

SortTypeEnum.title: SortTypeEnum
SortTypeEnum.description: The order in which to sort the brand kits. This can be either ascending (`asc`) or descending (`desc`).

items.description: The individual tags used to organize the brand kit.

updatedBy.description: The user who last updated the brand kit.

tags.title: Tags
tags.description: An array of tags associated with the brand kit to categorize its documents.

references.title: References
references.description: An array of references associated with the brand kit.

deletedAt.title: Deletedat
deletedAt.description: The date and time when the brand kit was deleted, formatted in ISO 8601.

id.title: Id
id.description: The unique identifier of the brand kit subsection.

name.title: Name
name.description: The name of the brand kit subsection.

order.title: Order
order.description: The order the subsection appears in the brand kit section. For example, if it is the first subsection,  `order` is `1`.

value.title: Value
value.description: The content of the subsection. This can be a string, object, or array depending on the type of content in the subsection.
value.example: The core purpose is to fuel the potential of active individuals with clean, nutrient-dense energy solutions.

intent.title: Intent
intent.description: The intent or prompt that instructs the AI in populating this subsection and retrieving information from existing brand knowledge based on uploaded brand documents.
intent.example: The core reason for the brand existence.

references.title: References
references.description: The brand documents the AI referenced to generate content for this subsection.

deletable.title: Deletable
deletable.description: Whether the subsection can be deleted.

verified.title: Verified
verified.description: Whether the subsection's content has been verified by the user.

createdOn.title: Createdon
createdOn.description: The date and time when the subsection was created, formatted in ISO 8601.

createdBy.title: Createdby
createdBy.description: The user who created the subsection.

updatedOn.title: Updatedon
updatedOn.description: The date and time when the subsection was last updated, formatted in ISO 8601.

updatedBy.title: Updatedby
updatedBy.description: The user who last updated the subsection.

aiEditable.title: Aieditable
aiEditable.description: Whether the subsection can be edited by AI. If set to `true`, the AI will overwrite the subsection's content value when brand documents are reprocessed.

tags.name: Brand kit
tags.description: The Brand kit API lets you create and retrieve brand kits within your organization. You can also retrieve or update sections—such as *Brand Context*, *Global Goals*, *Tone of Voice*, and *Glossary and Localization*—and retrieve or create subsections within those sections.
